title: Governed Reconciliation and Disclosure Separation
description: Reconcile eligible state internally and disclose findings only through a separate approval-bound operation with explicit temporal, contextual, privacy, consent, purpose, sensitivity, redaction, revocation, receipt, and recovery controls.
acceptance_criteria: Temporal and contextual eligibility deterministically excludes future, expired, retracted, superseded, historical, disputed, mismatched, and endpoint-ineligible state according to policy
Reconciliation and disclosure are separate operations with independent authority and receipts
Findings preserve full attributable lineage and UNKNOWN confidence remains deferred
Subject-facing disclosure requires subject request and external audiences require exact content-bound authority
Purpose, audience, consent, sensitivity, privacy, redaction, expiration, and revocation rules fail closed
Interrupted finding or disclosure persistence recovers by exact retry without duplicate effects or widened audience scope

evidence.description: Declared reconciliation and disclosure paths achieved runtime coverage.
